title: Mitochondrial oxidative DNA damage and exposure to particulate air pollution in mother-newborn pairs.
ehr_evidence: None mentioned; the study uses data from the ENVIRONAGE birth cohort.
summary: The study investigated the association between particulate matter (PM) exposure during pregnancy and mitochondrial 8-hydroxy-2'-deoxyguanosine (8-OHdG) levels, a biomarker for oxidative stress, in both mothers and newborns.
key_findings: PM10 exposure during the entire pregnancy was positively associated with maternal 8-OHdG levels.
PM2.5 exposure was significantly associated with increased maternal 8-OHdG levels during the entire pregnancy and third trimester.
PM10 exposure during the first and second trimesters was significantly associated with increased 8-OHdG levels in umbilical cord blood.
population: 224 mothers and 293 newborns (singletons) in East-Limburg Hospital, Belgium
limitations: Not specified in the provided text
